Safety Mar 24, 2026

No Transponder, No Warning: NTSB Details LaGuardia Alert Failure

NTSB says LaGuardia’s surface warning system was "unable to alert."

LaGuardia's surface detection system failed to generate alerts because the aircraft lacked a transponder signal, exposing a critical gap in airport safety infrastructure for non-equipped aircraft.

See Simple Flying →

Regulatory Mar 24, 2026

FAA Expands Swift 100R Engine STC

Amended STC broadens engine coverage for the unleaded avgas.

The expanded STC enables more aircraft models to utilize Swift 100R fuel, reducing operator dependency on traditional leaded avgas and accelerating the industry's transition to compliant fuel alternatives.

See AVweb →

Safety Mar 24, 2026

NTSB: A multitude of factors likely led to deadly LaGuardia collision

The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) held a second briefing, on March 24, 2026, that offered further details… The post NTSB: A multitude of factors likely led to deadly LaGuardia collision appeared first on AeroTime.

Multiple contributing factors rather than a single cause suggests systemic vulnerabilities in airport operations or traffic management that require comprehensive corrective actions beyond isolated procedural fixes.

See AeroTime Hub →

PAI
Regulatory Mar 24, 2026

SMS Declaration of Compliance

What the SMS Declaration of Compliance means for operators and what the FAA expects.

Operators must formally demonstrate their Safety Management System meets FAA standards through documented compliance declarations, establishing accountability for proactive safety oversight rather than reactive incident response.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

General Aviation Mar 24, 2026

NASA Selects Finalists in Student Aircraft Maintenance Competition

Editor’s note: This advisory was updated on March 24, 2026, to update the finalists list. NASA has selected seven student teams as finalists in the 2026 Gateways to Blue Skies Competition, giving them the resources to help address a critical challenge for U.S. aviation: aircraft maintenance. Chal...

NASA's maintenance competition targets a severe workforce shortage that directly impacts airline operations and safety, making student engagement critical to aviation's sustainability.

See NASA →

Drones Mar 24, 2026

ResilienX Receives FAA Waiver for Remote BVLOS Operations

ResilienX, Inc., a leader in aviation safety and BVLOS drone networks, announced today that it

FAA waivers enabling remote beyond-visual-line-of-sight operations signal regulatory acceptance of autonomous drone networks for critical infrastructure missions.

Drones Mar 24, 2026

When Wireless Networks Falter, Drones Can Provide Backup Connectivity, According to Stevens Researchers

Researchers at Stevens Institute of Technology have developed a drone-based system designed to provide temporary

Autonomous drone swarms can restore communication infrastructure during network outages, offering resilient disaster recovery without human operators.

See sUAS News →

PAI
Safety Mar 24, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS frameworks shift aviation from incident-driven corrections to predictive risk identification, fundamentally changing how operators allocate safety resources.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Drones Mar 24, 2026

America’s new 911 drone is always ready

Drones responding to 911 calls are no longer experimental. Across the US, police departments are already using Drone as First Responder (DFR) programs to get eyes on emergencies faster than patrol units. In many cities, drones arrive in under two minutes, well before officers. But even with that ...

Sub-two-minute drone response times are reshaping emergency dispatch priorities, forcing police to retrain dispatchers on when to send drones versus ground units.

See DroneDJ →

Air Cargo Mar 24, 2026

How Much Do Cargo Pilots Make Compared To Passenger Airline Pilots?

Passenger airline pilots can earn more, but being a cargo pilot is also a lucrative career.

Cargo pilots benefit from premium pay rates and scheduling flexibility despite lower baseline salaries, making the career financially competitive with passenger flying for those prioritizing work-life balance.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 24, 2026

NASA X-Ray Mission Gets Fresh Look at 2,000-Year-Old Supernova

NASA’s IXPE (Imaging X-ray Polarimetry Explorer) mission has taken a new observation of a supernova, RCW 86, helping fill in a fuller picture of what other telescopes have observed. When astronomers using NASA’s Chandra X-ray Observatory previously targeted RCW 86, they discovered that a large “c...

IXPE's polarimetry capabilities reveal previously undetectable magnetic field structures in supernova remnants, advancing our understanding of extreme astrophysical phenomena relevant to space-based instrumentation design.

See NASA →

▶
General Aviation Mar 24, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use t

Eastern Iowa Airport's mixed-use model supporting both flight training and commercial air carrier operations demonstrates how regional airports can diversify revenue streams and maintain economic viability.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Regulatory Mar 24, 2026

SparkPNT Enters the Next Chapter with Facet FP

Designed with the belief that users should have total control over the devices they own,

SparkPNT's partnership with Facet FP addresses the critical aviation need for independent, user-controlled positioning systems that reduce reliance on vulnerable GPS infrastructure.

See sUAS News →

Safety Mar 24, 2026

NTSB: LaGuardia Surface Alert System Did Not Trigger

Investigators said the fire truck involved did not have a transponder, and a stepped-on radio transmission remains under review.

The absence of transponder technology on airport ground vehicles created a critical blind spot in collision-avoidance systems, highlighting a potential safety gap in ground infrastructure modernization.

See AVweb →

Regulatory Mar 24, 2026

IAA Prosecutes Drone Operator for Impeding Investigation and Breach of Direction

The Irish Aviation Authority (IAA) brought a prosecution in the District Court against a drone operator for impeding an official investigation

Ireland's enforcement action signals regulators will pursue criminal prosecution against operators who obstruct official investigations, establishing meaningful deterrents beyond standard licensing violations.

See sUAS News →

▶
Safety Mar 24, 2026

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via taxi

Runway 24R exit taxiway design at LAX creates systematic incursion risks that require pilots to cross active runways, exposing a critical gap between standard procedures and physical infrastructure.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Commercial Mar 24, 2026

Here’s Why Airlines Keep Refurbishing Boeing 777-300ER Cabins Instead Of Replacing Them

Airlines refurbish their Boeing 777-300ER cabins as it offers a more cost-effective solution and improves the efficiency of an aircraft's lifecycle.

Cabin refurbishment extends 777-300ER economic viability during aircraft supply constraints, deferring costly fleet replacements until new aircraft deliveries accelerate.

See Simple Flying →

Regulatory Mar 24, 2026

FAA Updates GNSS Interference Guide Months After First Release

Update adds pilot procedures and expands detail on recognizing and reporting jamming and spoofing.

The revised guidance now equips pilots with actionable procedures to identify and report GNSS attacks, shifting responsibility from detection to active participation in combating emerging navigation threats.

See AVweb →

Aerospace Mar 24, 2026

La NASA presenta iniciativas para cumplir con la política espacial nacional de Estados Unidos

Read this news release in English here. Como parte de su evento “Ignition” (Encendido) celebrado el martes, la NASA anunció una serie de iniciativas transformadoras a nivel de toda la agencia, diseñadas para cumplir con la Política Espacial Nacional del presidente Donald J. Trump y promover el li...

NASA's agency-wide initiatives align with Trump administration space policy, signaling potential shifts in funding priorities and commercial partnership strategies affecting aerospace contractors.

See NASA →

Industry Mar 24, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across all aviation sectors offers immediate environmental gains while capacity-building technologies can enable sustainable growth without proportional emissions increases.

See MDPI →

Picture of the Day: March 24, 2026

A formation of antique aircraft in flight over Lady Liberty.

Vintage aircraft formation flights over iconic landmarks demonstrate continued airworthiness and passion for aviation heritage preservation among general aviation communities.

El Al cuts capacity by 95% as Israel limits Ben Gurion Traffic

El Al will slash its flight operations after Israel’s Transportation Ministry sharply limited traffic at Ben Gurion Airport near… The post El Al cuts capacity by 95% as Israel limits Ben Gurion Traffic appeared first on AeroTime.

Israel's capacity restrictions at Ben Gurion represent a systemic constraint that extends beyond El Al to affect the entire regional aviation network's recovery timeline.

Commercial Mar 24, 2026

The Striking Differences Cabin Crew Notice Between The Boeing 787 Dreamliner & Airbus A350

The battle of the long-haul giants.

Cabin crew productivity and passenger comfort differ significantly between aircraft designs, influencing airline operational costs and competitive advantages on premium routes.

Commercial Mar 24, 2026

Why This Monster Jet Engine Is Crushing Boeing’s Mounting Competition From Airbus

The Rolls-Royce UltraFan could be the powerplant of choice for next-generation aircraft.

The UltraFan's advanced architecture addresses fuel efficiency and emissions pressures that will define aircraft competitiveness through 2040s, potentially reshaping engine selection dynamics independent of airframe manufacturer decisions.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 24, 2026

The World's Most Affordable Transatlantic Business Class Routes You Can Book Today

Discover affordable lie-flat seats across the Atlantic on airlines like JetBlue, La Compagnie, and TAP for as low as $1,600.

New transatlantic business class pricing under $1,600 signals intensifying competition eroding premium cabin yield, forcing legacy carriers to defend market share against low-cost disruptors.

Regulatory Mar 24, 2026

Op-Ed: Repeated government shutdowns normalize aviation instability

U.S. Government shutdowns disrupt airport operations and a variety of private businesses, from airlines and bizjet operators to airport shops and restaurants. Please enjoy this Op-Ed contribution from Mohamed Yousuf, the CEO and founder of Smart Workforce AI. When government shutdowns occur, they...

Repeated shutdowns erode operational predictability across aviation's entire ecosystem, creating cumulative costs that extend beyond temporary FAA furloughs to supply chains and ancillary businesses.

See Runway Girl Network →

Commercial Mar 24, 2026

A Real-Life “Home Alone”? Brenda Song Blasts Alaska Airlines After Kids Separated From Parents

The carrier has apologised for the mix-up.

Operational errors in passenger handling highlight systemic risks in family-group management during boarding that could expose airlines to significant liability and reputational damage.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 24, 2026

Here's Why Airbus Doesn't Seem Fazed Over Boeing's Push For The 737 MAX 10

Airbus isn't losing sleep over Boeing's 737 MAX 10, and here's why.

Airbus's A321neo maintains competitive advantages in range, capacity, and fuel efficiency that reduce vulnerability to Boeing's MAX 10 offering despite near-term market pressures.

Commercial Mar 24, 2026

United Airlines To Complete Starlink WiFi Installations Across Entire Fleet By 2027

About a quarter of the carrier’s daily departures already feature the fast and free internet service.

United's aggressive Starlink rollout could force competitors to accelerate WiFi investments to remain competitive in the premium passenger experience segment.

NASA Research Proposes Technology to Seek Earth-Like Exoplanets

As NASA seeks to understand the mysteries of the universe, the agency is advancing technologies to locate and explore Earth-like planets far beyond our solar system. A key element of this research involves observing reflected light from exoplanets, which can reveal indicators of Earth-like featur...

NASA's exoplanet detection technology using reflected light analysis could drive innovations in advanced optical systems applicable to aerospace remote sensing and imaging.
